headline: Gender equality bill shelved after 3 drafts
context: The Gender Equality Act, drafted three times since 2019, was shelved again in 2025 after opposition from conservative groups.

cards.big: Gender Equality Act shelved 3 times since 2019 despite CEDAW obligations
cards.sub: Malaysia ratified CEDAW in 1995 but lacks comprehensive anti-discrimination law

cards.big: Federal Constitution Article 8(2) prohibits gender discrimination
cards.sub: No standalone legislation provides enforcement mechanisms for gender equality

cards.big: Malaysia ranked 104th of 146 countries on Gender Gap Index 2024
cards.sub: CEDAW committee issued 28 recommendations to Malaysia in its last review

cards.big: Women hold only 14.9% of parliamentary seats as of 2025
cards.sub: Gender wage gap persists at 18.3% across private sector employment

cards.big: Three drafts shelved means political will, not technical capacity, is lacking
Women's Aid Organisation called for bipartisan support in January 2025
